source_docs.title: Characterization of the single-cell transcriptional landscape by highly multiplex RNA-seq
STRT Supplementary Tables 1-2: TSO list and 96-well orientation

oligos.name: STRT template-switching oligo (TSO; STARTv2-1..96 / STRT-V2-n)
oligos.role: Barcoded template-switching helper oligo; introduces the 6-bp cell/well barcode and the universal (STRT-PCR) primer sequence at the 3′ end of the cDNA (5′ end of mRNA) via MMLV template switching

components.name: universal / STRT-PCR primer-binding sequence
components.role: constant upstream primer sequence (contains two deoxyuracil residues, ...GCAGUGCU..., that allow USER-enzyme degradation of the TSO before PCR)

components.name: riboguanine template-switch tail
components.role: three riboguanines (rGrGrG) that anneal to the non-templated dC tail added by reverse transcriptase

oligos.notes: Protocol (2012) sequence: 5′-AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTGCAGUGCUXXXXXXrGrGrG-3′ (U = deoxyuracil, folded to T here; rG folded to G). Original 2011 paper form (STRT-V2-n): 5′-AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTGCAGTGCTXXXXXXrGrGrG-3′ (no uracils). 96 error-correcting 6-bp barcodes (Supplementary Table 1): 1 TTTAGG, 2 ATTCCA, 3 GCTCAA, 4 CATCCC, 5 TTGGAC, 6 CTGTGT, 7 GGACAT, 8 CAAAGT, 9 AAGCGG, 10 AATAAA, 11 GAGGAG, 12 GGTACA, 13 AGCGAG, 14 GTCGGT, 15 ATTTGC, 16 AGGACT, 17 GCCCTC, 18 TCGTAA, 19 CCAGAC, 20 TATGTA, 21 ACAATA, 22 ATGCTT, 23 AGTTTA, 24 CACAAG, 25 ATCAAC, 26 TAGTCG, 27 TAGAGA, 28 GTCCCG, 29 TACTTC, 30 AAAGTT, 31 TAAGGG, 32 GTTGCC, 33 AAGTAC, 34 GATCTT, 35 TTAACT, 36 GCGAAT, 37 CCGCTA, 38 TGAAGC, 39 ATACAG, 40 CTTCTG, 41 GAGATC, 42 CCGACG, 43 CTCCAT, 44 AAAACG, 45 TAGCAT, 46 TCGGGT, 47 GTGGTA, 48 CCTAGA, 49 GGGTTT, 50 ATGGCG, 51 TTCATA, 52 AACGCC, 53 GGCTGC, 54 GCTGTG, 55 AGATGG, 56 GTAATG, 57 AGGGTC, 58 ATCTCT, 59 GCCTAG, 60 TCAAAG, 61 CATGAT, 62 TGTGCG, 63 GCAGGA, 64 TCTACC, 65 AGTCGT, 66 CGTGGC, 67 GCGTCC, 68 GAACGC, 69 ACTTAT, 70 TGGATG, 71 TATTGT, 72 ACGTTG, 73 GAATTA, 74 CCATCT, 75 TGATCA, 76 CGTATT, 77 CGGCAG, 78 GACACT, 79 TTCCGC, 80 CTCGCA, 81 GTATAC, 82 TGTCAC, 83 TGCGGA, 84 ACGAGC, 85 ACACCC, 86 CGCTTG, 87 TGCAAT, 88 CAACAA, 89 CTGAAA, 90 AACCTA, 91 ACCTGA, 92 TCACTT, 93 GGGCGA, 94 CGCACC, 95 CGAGTA, 96 CCTTTC.

oligos.name: STRT-V3-T30VN (RT oligo-dT primer)
oligos.role: Biotinylated anchored oligo-dT reverse-transcription primer that captures poly(A)+ mRNA and primes first-strand cDNA synthesis
oligos.notes: 2012 protocol: 5′-Bio-TTAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTCGACT30VN-3′; 2011 paper: 5′-biotin-AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTCGACT30VN-3′. 5′-biotinylated to prevent template switching at its 5′ end. Contains 30 dT (T30). Underlined GTCGAC is a SalI restriction site (used to release 3′ fragments from beads). 3′ 'VN' anchor: V = A/C/G, N = any base, positions the primer at the start of the poly(A) tail.

oligos.name: STRT-PCR
oligos.role: Biotinylated single primer used to amplify the pooled full-length barcoded cDNA (anneals to the universal sequence common to both TSO and RT primer)
oligos.notes: 5′-Bio-AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT-3′; 5′-biotin allows subsequent streptavidin-bead immobilization of the amplified cDNA.

oligos.name: SOLEXA-ADP2 (Illumina P2 adapter)
oligos.role: Double-stranded, forked/T-overhang adapter ligated to the A-tailed free (3′/fragmented) end of the cDNA; provides the P2 sequence for the library

components.name: SOLEXA-ADP2U (upper strand)
components.role: top adapter strand with a 3′ 'T' overhang that base-pairs with the cDNA 'A' overhang; carries the P2 (Solexa-P2) motif

components.name: SOLEXA-ADP2L (lower strand)
components.role: bottom adapter strand; both 5′ and 3′ ends phosphorylated (5′-PHO for ligation, 3′-PHO blocks ligation in the wrong orientation). Given in the source 3′→5′ as PHO-GTTCGTCTTCTGCCGTATGCTCGAGAAGGCTAG-PHO

oligos.notes: Annealed from 10 µM SOLEXA-ADP2U + 10 µM SOLEXA-ADP2L. Only adapter 2 (P2) is ligated on-bead; adapter 1 (P1) is added later by PCR to minimize adapter dimers.

oligos.name: P1-STRT
oligos.role: Library PCR forward primer; introduces the Illumina P1 flow-cell sequence via a tail appended to the STRT-PCR motif

components.name: STRT-PCR motif
components.role: anneals to the universal cDNA end

oligos.name: SOLEXA-P2
oligos.role: Library PCR reverse primer; amplifies from the ligated P2 adapter

oligos.name: STRT-SEQ-V3 (custom sequencing primer)
oligos.role: Custom Illumina sequencing primer read from the P1 side; anneals immediately upstream of the barcode so read 1 begins at the barcode
oligos.notes: 5′-AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTGCAGTGCT-3′. The standard Illumina sequencing primer cannot be used; this custom primer is required. Read = barcode(6) + GGG + insert.

final_library.source_label: STRT-seq final Illumina library (5′→3′ top strand)
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCT | AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT | GCAGTGCT | [CELL_BARCODE:6] | GGG | [CDNA] | A·GATCGGAAGAGC(adapter2) | CTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG(P2 revcomp)
annotation_lines: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCT  — Illumina P1 (introduced by P1-STRT in library PCR)
GCAGTGCT                   — TSO tag (from ...GCAGUGCU..., copied as T)
A GATCGGAAGAGC...          — dA tail + ligated SOLEXA-ADP2 (P2) adapter, read-through
CTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG     — reverse complement of Solexa-P2 (P2 end)

evidence.locator: STRT-seq final Illumina library (5′→3′ top strand)

library_generation.title: Cell capture & lysis
library_generation.summary: Single cells are sorted one-per-well into a 96-well plate preloaded with the biotinylated oligo-dT RT primer and STRT buffer, then frozen to lyse.
library_generation.note: Each well contains 400 nM STRT-V3-T30VN plus ~2,500 molecules of 8 synthetic spike-in control mRNAs (750–2,000 bp) for absolute molecule calibration. Freezing on dry ice contributes to lysis.
library_generation.product:
well: [ single cell lysate + spike-ins ]
5'-Bio-TTAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTCGACTTTT...TTTVN-3'  (oligo-dT RT primer)

mRNA:  5'- m7G-[transcript]-AAAA...AAA -3'

library_generation.title: mRNA capture & first-strand synthesis
library_generation.summary: The anchored oligo-dT primer anneals to the poly(A) tail and Superscript II reverse transcribes the mRNA, adding 3–6 non-templated C's at the cDNA 3′ end.
library_generation.note: Mn2+ in the RT mix improves template-switching efficiency. MMLV RT adds non-templated dC (typically CCC) at the 3′ end of the completed first strand (the 5′ end of the mRNA).
library_generation.product:
mRNA   5'- [transcript] -AAAAAAAA -3'
              |||||||| (reverse transcription)
cDNA 3'-CCC-[cDNA]------TTTTTTTTVN-AGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTCGACT...-Bio-5'
         ^^^ non-templated dC tail

library_generation.title: Template switching & barcoding
library_generation.summary: The barcoded TSO's 3′ riboG tail anneals to the dC tail; RT switches template and copies the TSO, appending the well barcode and universal primer sequence.
library_generation.note: A different TSO (barcode) is present in each of the 96 wells, so every cell's cDNA acquires a unique 6-bp barcode + universal upstream sequence at its 5′ end.
library_generation.product:
TSO:  5'-AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTGCAGUGCU-[BC6]-rGrGrG-3'
                                            anneals ||| 
cDNA 3'-...[cDNA]-CCC-3'  --template switch-->

first-strand cDNA (3'->5'):
3'-...[cDNA]-CCC-[BC6 comp]-UCGUGACGUGAGACGCAACTATGGTGGACGAA-5'

library_generation.title: Pool, USER cleavage & single-primer PCR
library_generation.summary: All 96 wells are pooled and purified; USER enzyme degrades the uracil-containing TSO, then the biotinylated STRT-PCR primer amplifies full-length barcoded cDNA (20 cycles).
library_generation.note: Single-primer PCR (both ends share the universal AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT sequence) reduces cell-to-cell amplification bias. USER digestion prevents leftover TSO from priming.
library_generation.product:
5'-Bio-AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTGCAGTGCT-[BC6]-GGG-[cDNA]-AAAA...-3'
3'-    TTCGTCACCATAGTTGCGTCTCACGTC.....-[BC6']-CCC-[cDNA]-TTTT...-5'
   (double-stranded, barcoded, full-length cDNA; biotin at 5' ends)

library_generation.title: Streptavidin bead immobilization
library_generation.summary: The biotinylated amplified cDNA is captured on MyOne C1 streptavidin beads.
library_generation.note: Immobilization enables subsequent on-bead fragmentation and enzymatic steps with easy washing.

library_generation.title: Fragmentation
library_generation.summary: Bead-bound cDNA is fragmented with dsDNA fragmentase, retaining the barcoded 5′ end on the bead.
library_generation.note: Fragmentation shortens the molecule so that only the barcode-proximal 5′ fragment is carried forward for sequencing.

library_generation.title: End repair & dA-tailing
library_generation.summary: Fragment ends are blunted (end repair) and a single 3′ A overhang is added with Klenow exo−.
library_generation.note: The dA overhang enables T/A ligation of the P2 adapter to the free (fragmented) end.

library_generation.title: P2 adapter ligation
library_generation.summary: The SOLEXA-ADP2 adapter (T overhang) is ligated to the A-tailed free end; SalI-HF simultaneously releases fragments from the bead.
library_generation.note: Only the P2 adapter is ligated here to avoid adapter dimers; SalI cleaves the SalI site in the RT-primer region to free the 3′ fragments.
library_generation.product:
5'-...GGG-[cDNA fragment]-A GATCGGAAGAGCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG-3'
                          T CTAGCCTTCTCGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC-5'
                          (ligated SOLEXA-ADP2 / P2)

library_generation.title: Library PCR (introduce P1)
library_generation.summary: P1-STRT (forward) and Solexa-P2 (reverse) primers amplify for 12 cycles, appending the Illumina P1 sequence.
library_generation.note: P1-STRT tails the universal STRT-PCR motif with the P1 flow-cell sequence, completing a sequenceable Illumina library.

library_generation.title: Size selection
library_generation.summary: The library is size-selected on a 2% E-gel, recovering the 200–400 bp range for sequencing.
library_generation.note: Final concentration ~1–2 ng/µl (~1 nM); molar concentration is used to set flow-cell cluster density.

library_sequencing.note: Mandatory custom sequencing primer read from the P1 side (the standard Illumina primer does not anneal). The primer's 3' end sits immediately upstream of the barcode, so the read begins with the 6-bp well/cell barcode, then the template-switch GGG, then the 5' end (TSS) of the transcript. Original paper reported ~55-bp reads on a Genome Analyzer IIx.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 (STRT-SEQ-V3, single-end from the P1 side)
           5'-AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTGCAGTGCT----------------------->   (read = BC6 + GGG + cDNA 5' end)
5'- ...GAGATCTAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTGCAGTGCT[BC6]GGG[cDNA]AGATCGGAAGAGCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'   (top / sense)
3'- ...CTCTAGATTCGTCACCATAGTTGCGTCTCACGTCACGA[BC6]CCC[cDNA]TCTAGCCTTCTCGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'   (bottom / antisense)
                                              ^ read starts here (barcode)

title: STRT-seq
description: Single-cell tagged reverse transcription (STRT) is a highly multiplexed, strand-specific single-cell RNA-seq method that sequences the 5′ ends of mRNAs. Ninety-six single cells are lysed in individual wells; poly(A)+ mRNA is reverse transcribed with a biotinylated tailed oligo-dT primer, and MMLV reverse transcriptase template-switching adds a well-specific 6-bp barcode plus a universal primer sequence at the 5′ end of each cDNA. All 96 barcoded cDNAs are pooled, amplified by single-primer PCR, immobilized on streptavidin beads, fragmented, end-repaired/A-tailed, ligated to an Illumina P2 adapter, and library-amplified to introduce the P1 sequence. Each Illumina read starts at the barcode, followed by the template-switch GGG, followed by the 5′ end of the transcript (transcription start site), yielding strand-specific, promoter-resolved quantitative expression profiles.

reference.label: Characterization of the single-cell transcriptional landscape by highly multiplex RNA-seq

original_publication.title: Characterization of the single-cell transcriptional landscape by highly multiplex RNA-seq

publication.statistical_model: Read counts normalized to transcripts per million (t.p.m.) without gene-length normalization (one sequenceable fragment per input mRNA); absolute mRNA molecule numbers per cell estimated by normalizing hit counts to spiked-in synthetic control mRNAs of known concentration.

throughput.summary: 96 single cells per library prepared in one incubation via early barcoding; two 96-cell sets prepared in parallel over ~3 days. ~110 million raw reads on 5 GAIIx lanes; ~241,000 mRNA molecules detected per cell on average.
throughput.cells: 96 single cells per library (92 cells reported: 48 ES cells, 44 MEFs, 4 empty wells; plus 96-well 10 pg RefRNA controls)
throughput.rna: ~241,000 mRNA molecules detected per cell on average (ES ~22,000; MEF ~505,000; negative controls ~812); ten-molecule limit of detection; 5–25% of mRNA converted to amplifiable cDNA

other.label: Detailed protocol
other.value: Islam et al., Nature Protocols 7:813–828 (2012), doi:10.1038/nprot.2012.022 — 'Highly multiplexed and strand-specific single-cell RNA 5′ end sequencing'

other.label: Spike-ins
other.value: 8 synthetic polyadenylated ArrayControl RNAs (750–2,000 bp) per well for absolute molecule counting

other.label: Key advantages
other.value: Strand-specific; identifies transcription start sites (single-cell CAGE); early barcoding lowers cost (~$200 per 96 cells) — but cannot resolve alternative splicing

other.label: Sequencing platform
other.value: Illumina Genome Analyzer IIx (also compatible with HiSeq 1000/2000, MiSeq, HiScanSQ) with custom STRT-SEQ-V3 primer

data_processing.summary: Reads are demultiplexed by the 6-bp well barcode, trimmed, and aligned to the mouse genome with Bowtie; reads per annotated feature are counted and normalized to transcripts per million (t.p.m.), and cells are then embedded in 2D via a Bray-Curtis kNN similarity graph laid out by a force-directed algorithm. Absolute mRNA molecule counts per cell are derived by normalizing hit counts to spiked-in synthetic control mRNAs of known concentration.
